title: MARS-seq2.0: Experimental and Analytical Pipeline for Single-Cell RNA Sequencing
author: Hadas Keren Shaul
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7hkhj4w

steps.title: Turn on the Bravo liquid-handling platform...
steps.instruction: Turn on the Bravo liquid-handling platform and wait for initialization to be completed. Load the ‘cell capture plate preparation’ protocol file for the 96ST head. The appropriate protocol file can be found in the Supplementary Software.

steps.title: Prepare cell capture plate master mixes...
steps.instruction: Prepare cell capture plate master mixes in four 96-well plates: lysis buffer ( 0.1 % volume Triton X-100 in nuclease-free water) supplemented with 0.1 U/μl RNase inhibitor (RNasin Plus) and 1 millimolar (mM) RT1 primer from group 1 (barcodes 1–96), group 2 (barcodes 97–192), group 3 (barcodes 193–288) or group 4 (barcodes 289–384). Barcode groups are listed in Supplementary Table 1. To prepare about twenty-four 384-well plates, use a multichannel pipette to mix 57.5 µL of lysis buffer with 5 µL of 12.5 nanomolar (nM) RT1 primer stock per well of a 96-well plate.

steps.title: Place the 96-well cell capture plate...
steps.instruction: Place the 96-well cell capture plate master mixes, a box containing filtered tips and the destination 384-well plates in the appropriate locations in the Bravo robot. The cell capture plate preparation script mixes the group 1 master mix plate, aspirates 2 µL from it and dispenses it into destination 384-well plate 1. Then 2 µL is again aspirated from group 1 master mix plate to be dispensed into the other destination 384-well plates. If more than three cell capture plates are prepared, the robotic script will pause and prompt the user to replace the filled destination plates with new plates. Once group 1 master mix has been added to all desired plates, the tips are replaced and the process is repeated for groups 2, 3 and 4. The entire process takes ~ 00:50:00 for 24 plates. See Fig. 3a for a schematic of the plates’ locations in this protocol.

steps.title: Seal the prepared cell capture plates...
steps.instruction: Seal the prepared cell capture plates and freeze at -20 °C until use. Cell capture plates can be kept at -20 °C for 1–2 years.

steps.title: Thaw the cell capture plates from...
steps.instruction: Thaw the cell capture plates from Step 4 and spin them down. Keep the plates on ice until sorting.

steps.title: Prepare a single-cell suspension for FACS (see ‘Biological materials’ section)
steps.instruction: Prepare a single-cell suspension for FACS (see ‘Biological materials’ section). We recommend gating on side scatter area (SSC-A) versus forward scatter area (FSC-A) to collect live cells, and then on forward scatter width (FSC-W) versus FSC-A to sort only singlets. ? TROUBLESHOOTING

steps.title: Calibrate the FACS machine for single-cell sorting into plates
steps.instruction: Calibrate the FACS machine for single-cell sorting into plates. See Supplementary Manual 1 for detailed information on single-cell sorting optimization.

steps.title: Place a cell capture plate in...
steps.instruction: Place a cell capture plate in the appropriate location in the FACS instrument and sort the desired population. We recommend leaving between two and four empty wells in each 384-well plate as a no-cell control during data analysis. CRITICAL STEP Keep the single-cell suspension on ice until FACS sorting. CRITICAL STEP The time from single-cell suspension to FACS sorting into the capture plates should be minimal. We recommend not exceeding 4–5 h.

steps.title: Immediately after sorting of each plate,...
steps.instruction: Immediately after sorting of each plate, seal the plate with an aluminum sealer, spin it down to ensure cell immersion into the lysis solution, snap-freeze on dry ice and store at -80 °C until further processing. CRITICAL STEP The sealer used must have the ability to sustain low temperatures; otherwise the seal can break off during freezing at −80 °C. CRITICAL STEP It is very important to spin down the cell capture plate immediately after singlecell sorting and keep it on dry ice until storage at −80 °C.

steps.title: Indexed FACS sorting is an optional element of the MARS-seq2
steps.instruction: Indexed FACS sorting is an optional element of the MARS-seq2.0 pipeline that allows correlating transcription and protein level by recording the level of surface markers for each sorted single cell.

steps.title: If you desire to record fluorescent...
steps.instruction: If you desire to record fluorescent marker levels for each single cell, activate the FACS Diva ‘index sorting’ function during single-cell sorting. See Supplementary Manual 3 for a detailed protocol on how to extract index sorting data.

steps.title: We recommend testing the single-cell sorting...
steps.instruction: We recommend testing the single-cell sorting efficiency in each new project or as a routine procedure following each sorting session. In Steps 13–19, one or two plates from the sorting session are used for testing of the single-cell sorting efficiency and not for library preparation.

steps.title: Thaw one of the sorted 384-well...
steps.instruction: Thaw one of the sorted 384-well plates from Step 9 for 00:05:00 at Room temperature and spin it down.

steps.title: Incubate the sorted 384-well plate for...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sorted 384-well plate for 00:03:00 at 72 °C to denature secondary structures and immediately transfer the plate to an ice block.

steps.title: Prepare a RT mix to add...
steps.instruction: Prepare a RT mix to add to 2–4 columns per plate according to the table below, and add 2 µL of RT mix per reaction well: %s %s

steps.title: Seal the plate, spin it down...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate, spin it down and incubate for 00:02:00 at 42 °C , for 00:50:00 at 45 °C , and for 00:05:00 at 85 °C .

steps.title: After RT, spin down the plate...
steps.instruction: After RT, spin down the plate and dilute the cDNA 1:20 as follows: (i) bring the volume in the columns in which RT was performed to 10 μl by adding 6 µL of EB8 to each well; (ii) add 1 µL of this diluted cDNA to 19 µL of EB8 in a separate dilution plate.

steps.title: Perform quantitative qPCR for mouse ActB or human GAPDH
steps.instruction: Perform quantitative qPCR for mouse ActB or human GAPDH. Prepare the qPCR master mix according to the table below (for duplicates): %s %s

steps.title: Run qPCR for 45 cycles as...
steps.instruction: Run qPCR for 45 cycles as shown in the table below: %s %s ? TROUBLESHOOTING

steps.title: Turn on the Bravo liquid handler,...
steps.instruction: Turn on the Bravo liquid handler, initialize and load the ‘RT mix addition’ script.

steps.title: Thaw the 384-well plates (from Step...
steps.instruction: Thaw the 384-well plates (from Step 9) to be processed at Room temperature for 00:05:00 and spin down. Keep on ice until the next step. Pre-heat a 384-well thermal cycler to 95 °C .

steps.title: Prepare an RT reaction mix sufficient...
steps.instruction: Prepare an RT reaction mix sufficient for the number of plates to be processed: %s %s

steps.title: Divide the RT mix among two...
steps.instruction: Divide the RT mix among two 8-well strips, 16 µL per well, and place the strips on a 4 °C , 96-well CPAC heating/cooling unit in the Bravo robot.

steps.title: Evaporation
steps.instruction: Evaporation . Remove the aluminum seal of the plate (Step 23) and place it at 95 °C for 00:03:00 for complete evaporation of the lysis buffer. Immediately transfer the plate to a cooling 384-well block for RT reaction mix addition using the robot. CRITICAL STEP Evaporation time should be adjusted depending on the thermal cycler used (typically between 3 and 4 min) in order to ensure complete evaporation.

steps.title: Activate the RT reaction mix addition script
steps.instruction: Activate the RT reaction mix addition script. This automated protocol adds 0.5 µL from the RT reaction mix (Step 24) to each well in the 384-well plate (placed on a 4 °C , 384-well CPAC heating/ cooling unit). The tips are replaced and the process is repeated until all wells in the entire 384-well plate are supplemented with RT reaction mix. The entire process takes 17 min per 384-well plate. See Fig. 3b for a schematic of the plates’ locations in this protocol. In the RT reaction, the final Triton X-100 concentration is 0.4 % volume and the RT primer concentration is 4 nanomolar (nM) .

steps.title: Following RT mix addition, seal the...
steps.instruction: Following RT mix addition, seal the plate, spin down and transfer it to a 384-well thermal cycler for the RT program ( 00:02:00 at 00:02:00 , 00:50:00 at 50 °C , and 00:05:00 at 85 °C ).

steps.title: Spin down the 384-well plate after...
steps.instruction: Spin down the 384-well plate after RT, and keep it on ice until the next step. Load the ‘Exonuclease I addition’ Bravo script.

steps.title: Prepare an exonuclease I reaction mix...
steps.instruction: Prepare an exonuclease I reaction mix sufficient for the number of plates to be processed: %s %s

steps.title: Divide the exonuclease I mix among...
steps.instruction: Divide the exonuclease I mix among two 8-well strips, 32 µL per well, and place the strips in a 4 °C , 96-well CPAC heating/cooling unit in the Bravo robot.

steps.title: Activate the ‘Exonuclease I addition’ script
steps.instruction: Activate the ‘Exonuclease I addition’ script. This automated protocol adds 1 µL from the exonuclease reaction mix to each well in the 384-well plate (placed in a 4 °C 384-well CPAC heating/cooling unit). The entire process takes 17 min per 384-well plate. See Fig. 3b for a schematic of the plates’ locations in this protocol.

steps.title: Following Exonuclease addition, seal the plate,...
steps.instruction: Following Exonuclease addition, seal the plate, spin down and transfer to a 384-well thermal cycler for the exonculease program ( 00:30:00 at 37 °C and 00:10:00 at 80 °C ).

steps.title: Load the ‘Pooling’ Bravo script
steps.instruction: Load the ‘Pooling’ Bravo script. The pooling protocol pools together 384 single cells that are to be amplified together as an amplification batch. Prepare a 96-well pooling plate: dispense 55 µL of 0.1 % volume Triton X-100 into each well in row H of a clean 96-well plate for tip pre-wash and place it in the appropriate location in the Bravo robot (destination 96-well plate).

steps.title: Spin down the 384-well plate after...
steps.instruction: Spin down the 384-well plate after the exonculease program and place it in the appropriate location in the Bravo robot. Activate the ‘pooling’ script: the process begins by pre-washing the tips and then aspirating the contents of the 384-well plate and dispensing them into row A of the 96-well destination plate. This is the first step of the pooling, which results in 12 wells in a 96-well plate (1 row), each well containing single cells from 2 columns of the 384-well plate. If more than one 384-well plate is pooled, tips will be replaced and the second 384-well plate will be pooled into row B of the 96-well destination plate, and so on. The process takes 15 min per plate. See Fig. 3c for a schematic of the plates’ locations in this protocol.

steps.title: Spin down the 96-well plate containing the pooled cDNA
steps.instruction: Spin down the 96-well plate containing the pooled cDNA.

steps.title: Purify the product with 0.9 volumes of AMPure XP beads
steps.instruction: Purify the product with 0.9 volumes of AMPure XP beads: add 86.4 µL to each well and pipette to mix. Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature and transfer the contents of each row to a LoBind Eppendorf tube. Discard the 96-well plate used for pooling. Continue with AMPure XP cleanup in the LoBind Eppendorf tube and elute in 19 µL of EB8. Transfer 0.9 µL of the pooled sample to 19.1 µL of EB8 for QC1 (Box 1), and 17 µL to the first column in a new 96-well plate for the next step. At the end of this process, all single cells in each 384-well plate are transferred into one well in a 96-well plate. If processing several 384-well plates in parallel, each plate is represented in one well in a 96-well plate, one after the other in the same column. CRITICAL STEP All AMPure XP cleanups are performed according to the manufacturing protocol. The amount of beads added in each step and the volume used for elution are indicated in the specific step. CRITICAL STEP From this step onward, the protocol is done in a 96-well plate, moving from one column to the next in each step. Each 384-well plate is represented in one well (one pool). ? TROUBLESHOOTING Box 1: Experimental QC by qPCR of a housekeeping gene See instructions on how to perform qPCR in Steps 20 and 21. This is an important measurement for estimating library quality: QC1: an estimation of the amount of starting material (Step 40). QC1 is performed after cells in one plate (or amplification batch) are barcoded, have undergone RT and are pooled together before the second-strand-synthesis step. Cells with high RNA content are expected to yield a lower CT value than cells with a lower RNA content (Anticipated results). QC2: a measurement of library linear amplification (see Step 53). QC2 is performed after the pooled single cells undergo in vitro transcription and reverse transcription (see Anticipated results). QC3: a measurement for final library quantity (see Step 56). QC3 is performed on the final amplified library (see Anticipated results). CRITICAL Primers for qPCR should be designed for a housekeeping gene on the 3ʹ end of the transcript (no more than 200 bp upstream of the polyA site). Recommended primer sequences for mouse and human are provided (Reagent setup). CRITICAL Although we recommend performing the above QC steps in order to maintain a foolproof process in the long run, these QC measurements can be skipped or sampled sporadically.

steps.title: Convert the pooled cDNA to double-stranded...
steps.instruction: Convert the pooled cDNA to double-stranded DNA using a second-strand-synthesis kit in which the enzyme is diluted 1:8 in nuclease-free water: %s %s CRITICAL STEP Mix the second-strand-synthesis reaction well with a 20-μl pipette.

steps.title: Spin down the plate and incubate...
steps.instruction: Spin down the plate and incubate at 16 °C for 02:00:00 .

steps.title: Purify the product with 1.4 volumes of AMPure XP beads
steps.instruction: Purify the product with 1.4 volumes of AMPure XP beads: add 10 µL of EB8 and 42 µL of AMPure XP beads. Continue with AMPure XP cleanup and elute in 8 µL of EB8. Do not transfer the supernatant to a new well; IVT is performed with the beads.

steps.title: Linearly amplify the cDNA by IVT
steps.instruction: Linearly amplify the cDNA by IVT. Prepare the following reaction mix and add 12 µL of IVT mix per well: %s %s

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C overnight
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C overnight. CRITICAL STEP Following IVT incubation overnight, the AMPure XP beads sink to the bottom. This is normal.

steps.title: Following IVT, add 10 µL of...
steps.instruction: Following IVT, add 10 µL of Turbo DNase I mix to digest the DNA template: %s %s

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Purify the aRNA product with 1
steps.instruction: Purify the aRNA product with 1.2 volumes of AMPure XP beads: add 20 µL of EB7.5 and 60 µL of AMPure XP beads. Continue with AMPure XP cleanup. Elute with 19 µL of EB7.5. Transfer 18 µL to a well in a new column in the same 96-well plate. CRITICAL STEP Make sure to avoid transferring the beads, as this will interfere with the next step.

steps.title: CRITICAL The aRNA (Step 49) is...
steps.instruction: CRITICAL The aRNA (Step 49) is fragmented by chemical fragmentation into a median size of ~300 nucleotides by 00:03:00 of incubation at 70 °C in Zn2+ RNA fragmentation solution. CRITICAL If aRNA (Step 49) was frozen, thaw the 96-well plate on ice and spin down before the next step.

steps.title: Add 2 µL of 10× Zn2+...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of 10× Zn2+ fragmentation reagent to the aRNA from Step 49.

steps.title: Purify the product with 2 volumes of AMPure XP beads
steps.instruction: Purify the product with 2 volumes of AMPure XP beads: add 44 µL of AMPure XP beads. Continue with AMPure XP cleanup. Elute with 5.5 µL of EB7.5. Transfer 5 µL to a well in new column and continue to ligation. CRITICAL STEP Drying time of the beads is considerably shorter than that used in the AMPure XP cleanup in other steps. CRITICAL STEP Make sure not to transfer beads in the elution step, as this will interfere with the next step.

steps.title: Ligate a plate adaptor to the...
steps.instruction: Ligate a plate adaptor to the fragmented RNA (RNA:ssDNA ligation): select a unique plate adaptor (Supplementary Table 2) for each library to be sequenced together and record it.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of ligation plate...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of ligation plate adaptor ( 100 micromolar (µM) ) to the purified fragmented RNA (from Step 55) and incubate for 00:03:00 in a thermal cycler pre-heated to 70 °C . Transfer immediately to a cooling block.

steps.title: Prepare a ligation master mix in...
steps.instruction: Prepare a ligation master mix in a LoBind Eppendorf tube by adding the ingredients below according to the order specified. The master mix should contain all the reagents, except for the fragmented RNA + ligation adaptor from Step 57. %s %s CRITICAL STEP When preparing a mix for ligation, follow these guidelines: (i) vortex and spin down after addition of each reagent, (ii) place DMSO and PEG at 37 °C until use, (iii) work very slowly with PEG to ensure the correct volume is added and (iv) store ATP in small aliquots at −80 °C.

steps.title: Add 14
steps.instruction: Add 14.1 µL from the ligation mix to the aRNA and ligation adaptor (from Step 57), mix very slowly until homogeneous and spin down the plate.

steps.title: Incubate at 22 °C for 02:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 22 °C for 02:00:00 .

steps.title: Purify the ligation product with 1
steps.instruction: Purify the ligation product with 1.5 volumes of AMPure XP beads: add 80 µL of EB7.5 + 100 µL of AMPure XP beads. Continue with AMPure XP cleanup. Elute with 13.7 µL of EB7.5 and transfer 13.2 µL to a well in a new column of the plate. CRITICAL STEP Allow a longer magnetization time for the AMPure XP beads (7–8 min). CRITICAL STEP Drying time of the AMPure XP beads may be longer (7–8 min).

steps.title: CRITICAL The ligated product is reverse-transcribed...
steps.instruction: CRITICAL The ligated product is reverse-transcribed with a primer complementary to the ligated adaptor.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of 25 micromolar...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of 25 micromolar (µM) RT2 primer to the purified ligated aRNA (from Step 61), mix by pipetting, seal and spin down the plate. You should have 14.2 µL per well.

steps.title: Prepare the RT mix according to...
steps.instruction: Prepare the RT mix according to the table below (omitting the ligated product + RT primer) and add 5.8 µL of the mix per reaction well: %s %s

steps.title: Seal the plate, spin down and...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate, spin down and incubate it for 00:02:00 at 42 °C , 00:50:00 at 45 °C , and 00:05:00 at 85 °C .

steps.title: Purify the cDNA with 1.5 volumes of AMPure XP beads
steps.instruction: Purify the cDNA with 1.5 volumes of AMPure XP beads: add 10 µL of EB8 and 45 µL of AMPure XP beads. Continue with AMPure XP cleanup and elute in 25 µL of EB8. Transfer 24 µL to a new column. Take 1.2 µL of the cDNA and add it to 18.8 µL of EB8 for QC2 (Box 1). ? TROUBLESHOOTING

steps.title: The library is completed by amplifying the cDNA using PCR
steps.instruction: The library is completed by amplifying the cDNA using PCR: transfer 11.5 µL of cDNA (Step 67) to a well in a new column and add 12.5 µL of KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix + 1 µL of 25 micromolar (µM) enrichment primer mix (Reagent setup).

steps.title: Run a PCR program as follows
steps.instruction: Run a PCR program as follows: %s %s CRITICAL STEP The optimal number of PCR cycles is a tradeoff between insufficient amplification and too much amplification (which can cause PCR bias). The number of cycles is dependent on the cell type used and should be calibrated for each project. Typically, the number of amplification cycles required for enrichment is between 11 and 17. In the table above, we have used 14 cycles (2 cycles at 57 °C and 12 cycles at 65 °C annealing temperature) as an example. If needed, the PCR can be repeated with an adjusted cycle number (altering the number of cycles performed at 65 °C annealing temperature) using the cDNA backup. CRITICAL STEP Tracking QC values in several experiments from the same cell type can help in estimating, empirically, the necessary enrichment. As QC2 (Step 67) indicates the amount of material in the sample before the final amplification, it can link the number of PCR cycles done to the final library concentration obtained at the end of the process.

steps.title: Purify the amplified library with 0
steps.instruction: Purify the amplified library with 0.7 volumes of AMPure XP beads: add 35 µL of EB8 and 42 µL of AMPure XP beads. Continue with AMPure XP cleanup. Elute with 20 µL of EB8. Transfer 19 µL of the final library to a new well. Take 2 µL of the final library and add it to 38 µL of EB8 for QC3 (Box 1).

steps.title: CRITICAL All libraries should be carefully examined before sequencing
steps.instruction: CRITICAL All libraries should be carefully examined before sequencing.

steps.title: Library concentration
steps.instruction: Library concentration . Measure the final library concentration (ng/μl) using the dsDNA HS Assay Kit and a Qubit fluorometer. A typical library should yield between 1 and 10 ng/μl of dsDNA. CRITICAL STEP For better accuracy, it is recommended to use 2 µL of the library for Qubit measurement. ? TROUBLESHOOTING

steps.title: Library profile
steps.instruction: Library profile . Evaluate the library profile and the mean molecule size using a 2200 TapeStation instrument or equivalent. ? TROUBLESHOOTING

steps.title: CRITICAL Successful libraries (passing QC checks) can be sequenced
steps.instruction: CRITICAL Successful libraries (passing QC checks) can be sequenced.

steps.title: Calculate the molarity of each pool,...
steps.instruction: Calculate the molarity of each pool, using the following formula: where [ng/μl] is the concentration obtained with the Qubit assay (Step 74) and [bp] is the library profile peak obtained with TapeStation (Step 75).

steps.title: Dilute each library pool to 4...
steps.instruction: Dilute each library pool to 4 nanomolar (nM) (or another equivalent molar concentration of the pools) with EB8 and mix the different pools in equal ratios.

steps.title: Libraries of 20–40 384-well plates can...
steps.instruction: Libraries of 20–40 384-well plates can be mixed and sequenced together on a NextSeq 500 High Output v2 kit flow cell to yield ~0.5 × 10 9 reads.

steps.title: Sequence libraries with the following parameters
steps.instruction: Sequence libraries with the following parameters: rd1 = 75 bp, rd2 = 15 bp, no indices.

steps.title: CRITICAL Detailed instructions on how to use the MARS-seq2
steps.instruction: CRITICAL Detailed instructions on how to use the MARS-seq2.0 analytical pipeline and create a single-cell database (SCDB) can be found in the MARS-seq2.0 pipeline user manual on our website http://compgenomics.weizmann.ac.il/tanay/?page_id=672. See Supplementary Manual 2.

steps.title: Make sure you have the correct...
steps.instruction: Make sure you have the correct SCDB directory structure in your Linux storage.

steps.title: Make sure that the parameters defined in scdb/config/config
steps.instruction: Make sure that the parameters defined in scdb/config/config.txt are correct.

steps.title: Add the metadata on the sequenced...
steps.instruction: Add the metadata on the sequenced library according to the SCDB as described in detail in the user manual.

steps.title: Add the
steps.instruction: Add the .fastq files (raw reads) obtained at Step 80 to the SCDB.

steps.title: Run the pipeline locally or on...
steps.instruction: Run the pipeline locally or on an SGE (sun grid engine) cluster.

steps.title: Sorted cell capture plates can be...
steps.instruction: Sorted cell capture plates can be kept at −80 °C for several years.

steps.title: Pre-heat a 384-well thermal cycler to 72 °C
steps.instruction: Pre-heat a 384-well thermal cycler to 72 °C .

steps.title: Prepare an ERCC spike-in mix (1:80,000)...
steps.instruction: Prepare an ERCC spike-in mix (1:80,000) in three steps to use in the RT reaction mix above. First, prepare a 1:1,000 dilution by adding 9 µL of EB7.5 to a 1:100 ERCC aliquot. Then, prepare a 1:10,000 dilution by mixing 2 µL of 1:1,000 ERCC with 18 µL of EB7.5. Last, prepare a 1:80,000 dilution by mixing 5 µL of 1:10,000 ERCC with 35 µL of EB7.5.

steps.title: Samples can be frozen at -20...
steps.instruction: Samples can be frozen at -20 °C until further processing (we have stored samples at this stage for up to 1 year).

steps.title: Using a multi-channel pipette with six...
steps.instruction: Using a multi-channel pipette with six barrels, transfer the contents of the last six wells, 48 µL in each well, to the first six wells in each row of the 96-well plate. This is the second step of the pooling, which results in 96 μl in each of the six wells.

steps.title: Samples can be frozen at -20...
steps.instruction: Samples can be frozen at -20 °C until further processing (we have stored samples at this stage for up to 1 year).

steps.title: Samples can be frozen at -80...
steps.instruction: Samples can be frozen at -80 °C until further processing (we have stored samples at this stage for up to 1 year).

steps.title: Incubate for exactly 00:03:00 in a...
steps.instruction: Incubate for exactly 00:03:00 in a thermal cycler pre-heated to 70 °C . Transfer immediately to a cooling block and add 2 µL of stop solution.

steps.title: Cool for 00:01:00 – 00:02:00 and spin down
steps.instruction: Cool for 00:01:00 – 00:02:00 and spin down.

steps.title: Incubate the plate for 00:03:00 in...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate for 00:03:00 in a thermal cycler pre-heated to 72 °C . Transfer immediately to a cooling block. Spin down the plate.

steps.title: PAUSE POINT Samples can be frozen...
steps.instruction: PAUSE POINT Samples can be frozen at -20 °C until further processing (we have stored samples at this stage for up to 1 year).

steps.title: Vortex and spin down
steps.instruction: Vortex and spin down. The remaining half of the cDNA can be kept as a backup at -20 °C .
